title: Best Herbal Teas to Reduce Sunburn Inflammation
intro_desc: Herbal teas for sunburn are a natural remedy that helps to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with sunburned skin. These teas are made from various herbs and plants that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling. The best herbal teas for sunburn include Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Aloe barbadensis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Aloe vera, Symphytum officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, Arnica montana, and Hamamelis virginiana. If left untreated, sunburn can lead to blistering, scarring, and even long-term damage to the skin, causing discomfort, pain, and potentially, emotional distress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a great remedy for sunburn because it has many healing properties. When we get sunburned, our skin becomes red, sore, and inflamed. Aloe barbadensis tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness. It also has soothing and calming effects that ease the pain and discomfort caused by sunburn. The tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, like vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the skin from damage and promote healing. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea can also help to replenish the skin's natural moisture barrier, which can become dry and damaged due to sunburn. This can lead to faster healing and a reduced risk of peeling and scarring. Additionally, aloe barbadensis tea has antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote the healing of sunburned skin.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for sunburn because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain, swelling, and damage to skin cells caused by UV radiation.

Flavonoids, also known as Milk Thistle, are good for sunburn because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also contain antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by UV rays.
Aloin is good for sunburn because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and pain caused by sunburn, while also promoting skin healing and preventing blistering.
Aloe-emodin is good for sunburn because it helps reduce inflammation and pain, and also promotes healing and regeneration of damaged skin cells.
Tannins are good for sunburn because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect damaged skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain caused by sunburn.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for sunburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Silybum marianum herbal tea that can help with sunburn, you'll need some dried milk thistle flowers, a teapot, a cup, and some boiling water. First, take one teaspoon of dried milk thistle flowers and place it in a teapot or a cup. Next, pour boiling water over the flowers, making sure they are completely covered. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flowers to release their healing properties into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into another cup, discarding the flowers. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to let the tea cool down before applying it to the sunburned area. To use the tea as a compress, soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to the affected skin for 15-20 minutes, repeating the process as needed. It's essential to note that while milk thistle tea may provide relief from sunburn, it's not a substitute for medical treatment. Always consult a healthcare professional for proper advice on treating sunburn.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea has been used for centuries to help soothe sunburned skin. This is because it contains special compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling caused by sunburn. When you drink Calendula officinalis tea, these compounds are released into your body, helping to calm the skin from the inside out. The tea also contains antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from further damage and promote healing. Additionally, Calendula officinalis tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection in sunburned skin. This can lead to faster recovery and less discomfort. Drinking Calendula officinalis tea may also help to cool the skin, providing relief from the heat and itching associated with sunburn. As a result, Calendula officinalis tea can be a useful addition to your sunburn care routine.

Kaempferol is good for sunburn because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and protect the skin from damage caused by UV rays.

Tannins, also known as Milk Thistle, are good for sunburn because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and damage caused by UV radiation.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for sunburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for sunburn relief, start by gathering dried Silybum marianum flowers or seeds, which you can purchase at a health food store or grow yourself. Clean and dry the flowers or seeds thoroughly before using them. Next, boil water in a pot and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add a teaspoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers or seeds to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. You can also add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Finally, let the tea cool down before applying it to the sunburned skin as a compress or bath. Silybum marianum has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and soothe sunburned skin, while also promoting healing and skin regeneration.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to soothe sunburn. The tea made from its leaves and flowers has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling caused by sun exposure. When applied topically as a compress or soaked in water to make a tea, the active compounds in Hypericum perforatum can help to cool down the skin, ease pain, and promote healing. The tea also contains antioxidants that protect the skin from further damage and promote the growth of new skin cells, which can help to speed up the recovery process. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for skin repair.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea for sunburn relief, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Silybum marianum flowers, boiling water, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. First, boil fresh water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then reduce the heat to a low simmer. Next, place one teaspoon of dried Silybum marianum flowers into the tea infuser or directly into the cup. Pour the boiling water over the flowers, making sure to fully saturate them. Allow the tea to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength. Strain the tea into a separate container using the infuser or a spoon, and discard the used flowers. Once the tea has cooled slightly, you can drink it as is or add a sweetener like honey to taste. It's recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the Silybum marianum tea per day to help alleviate sunburn symptoms, such as redness and discomfort.

Aloe vera also has antioxidants that protect the skin from damage caused by the sun's UV rays. This helps to prevent blisters and peeling, and promotes faster healing. When you drink aloe vera tea, it goes inside your body and helps to reduce the pain and discomfort from sunburn. It also helps to hydrate the skin and promote the growth of new skin cells, which is especially important when your skin is damaged from the sun.

You can find these in health food stores or online. Begin by boiling a cup of water, then add a tablespoon of the dried flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the tea to infuse and release its healing properties. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. The anti-inflammatory properties in Silybum marianum can help to reduce redness and pain, while its antioxidant properties can aid in the healing process. You can also add a few ice cubes to the tea for an extra cooling effect. Reapply the tea as needed, but be sure to test a small area of skin first to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the herb.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe sunburned skin. The plant contains powerful antioxidants that fight off free radicals, which cause inflammation and damage to the skin. When applied topically or taken as a tea, it can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with sunburn. Echinacea also has anti-inflammatory properties that can speed up the healing process and promote new skin growth. Additionally, it has antimicrobial properties that can prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for the skin to heal. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea can also help boost the immune system, which can aid in the body's natural recovery process. Its cooling effect can also provide relief from the heat and discomfort caused by sunburn, making it a popular natural remedy for sunburn sufferers.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering a handful of dried milk thistle flowers and leaves. You can find this herb in health food stores or online. Next, boil a cup of water in a pot and then let it cool for a minute or two. Add the dried herb to the water and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes, or until the liquid has turned a light yellow color. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the herb. You can add honey or lemon juice to taste, but it's recommended to drink the tea plain. The tea should be consumed within 24 hours of making it. It's essential to note that Silybum marianum tea may not provide immediate relief from sunburn, but it can help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing over time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe sunburned skin. The tea is made from the flowers of the arnica plant, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to sunburned skin, the tea can help reduce redness and swelling. The antioxidants in arnica montana tea can also help repair damaged skin cells and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, arnica montana tea can help make sunburned skin feel cooler and more comfortable. Additionally, arnica montana tea can help prevent blistering and peeling, which can make sunburned skin even more painful. Drinking arnica montana tea internally can also help boost the immune system, which can help the body recover from sunburn more quickly. The tea is easy to make by steeping the dried arnica flowers in hot water, and it can be applied directly to the skin or taken internally as a drink.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering a handful of dried milk thistle flowers or leaves from the Silybum marianum plant. You can buy this herb from a health store or grow it yourself in a well-drained area with full sun. Next, combine the dried herb with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbal goodness to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the herb particles. Once the tea has cooled down, you can apply it to the affected area as a compress or soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the sunburned skin for 15-20 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis tea, also known as rosemary tea, is a soothing remedy for sunburned skin. Rosemary tea also has antiseptic properties that prevent infection and promote healing. Additionally, it can help ease the itch and discomfort associated with sunburned skin. Drinking rosemary tea may also provide relief from the internal discomfort caused by sunburn, such as fever and fatigue. The tea's cooling effect can also help reduce the heat and itching associated with sunburned skin, making it feel more relaxed and calm.

remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for sunburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering a handful of Silybum marianum leaves and flowers. You can grow this plant in your garden or find it at a local health food store. Next, rinse the leaves and flowers with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Then, dry them completely with a paper towel. Once dry, add one teaspoon of the dried Silybum marianum leaves and flowers to a cup of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup. Allow the tea to cool before drinking it. It is recommended to drink 2-3 cups of the tea a day to help soothe and cool sunburned skin. You can also add aloe vera or other soothing herbs to enhance the tea's cooling effect.

Horsetail tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help cool the skin, reduce redness and swelling, and promote healing. The antioxidants in the tea, specifically silicic acid, can also help repair damaged skin cells and improve skin elasticity. remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal teas for sunburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering dried seeds of the plant, also known as milk thistle. Begin by boiling one liter of water in a pot. Then, add a tablespoon of dried seeds to the boiling water and let it simmer for five to seven minutes. After the simmering time is up, turn off the heat and let the tea steep for another five minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the seeds. Allow the tea to cool down before drinking it. For sunburn relief, drink one to two cups of the tea per day. You can also add a tablespoon of aloe vera gel or honey to the tea for extra soothing properties. When using this herbal tea, remember to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago lanceolata tea, also known as Plantain tea, has been used for centuries to treat sunburn. The leaves of the Plantago lanceolata plant contain compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. When you drink Plantago lanceolata tea or apply it topically, these compounds are absorbed into your skin, reducing inflammation and redness. The tea also has anti-oxidant properties that help to protect your skin from damage caused by the sun's UV rays. Additionally, Plantago lanceolata tea has a cooling effect, which can help to relieve the discomfort and itching associated with sunburn. This makes it a popular remedy for people who spend a lot of time outdoors, such as gardeners, hikers, and beachgoers. The tea is also rich in vitamins A and C, which are essential for skin health and can help to speed up the healing process. Drinking Plantago lanceolata tea can also help to prevent sunburn by protecting your skin from further damage.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering a handful of fresh Silybum marianum leaves or use 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves. If you're using fresh leaves, you'll need to dry them first by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once dried, place the Silybum marianum leaves in a teapot or heat-resistant cup, and pour in 1 cup of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their medicinal properties. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can now drink the Silybum marianum tea warm or at room temperature, ideally 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate sunburn symptoms such as redness, inflammation, and pain. You can also add honey or lemon to taste.

remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal teas for sunburn
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Silybum marianum herbal tea that helps with sunburn, start by gathering dried Silybum marianum flowers or buying them from a health store. If using fresh flowers, dry them by spreading them out in a single layer on a baking sheet and letting them sit in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for a few hours, or by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once your Silybum marianum flowers are dry, place one teaspoon of them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour in one cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a separate cup using the tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add a bit of honey to sweeten the tea if you like. Drink this tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm sunburned skin, and also to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

supplementary_best: To remedy sunburn, the best combination of herbal teas is 'Aloe barbadensis', 'Calendula officinalis', and 'Arnica montana'. 'Aloe barbadensis' and 'Aloe vera' are often confused, but 'Aloe barbadensis' is the more effective and safer option for internal use. This herb contains compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by sunburn. 'Calendula officinalis' is also known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with sunburn. 'Arnica montana' has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that accelerate the healing process of sunburned skin. When combined, these three herbs create a powerful blend that not only soothes and calms sunburned skin but also accelerates the healing process, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
supplementary_avoid: If you have sunburn, it's best to avoid herbal teas that can make it worse or cause more irritation. One such tea is Peppermint (Mentha piperita). The cooling sensation of peppermint might feel nice, but it can actually increase blood flow to the skin, which may make your sunburn feel worse. Ginger (Zingiber officinale) tea is another one to avoid, as its anti-inflammatory properties can interact with the skin's natural inflammatory response and potentially make your sunburn more painful. Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) tea, while often used for calming, can cause allergic reactions in some people, which may worsen sunburn symptoms. Also, avoid Sage (Salvia officinalis) tea, as it can dry out the skin further, making your sunburn even more uncomfortable. Finally, Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) tea is not recommended, as it can increase fluid retention and make your skin look more inflamed.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a popular natural remedy for sunburn relief. It's rich in a compound called glycerin, which helps to soothe and moisturize the skin. When applied to sunburned skin, aloe vera gel can reduce redness and swelling, and even help to prevent blisters. This is because aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory properties that calm the skin and promote healing.  Another natural remedy for sunburn is oatmeal. Oatmeal has anti-inflammatory and cooling properties that can help to reduce heat and discomfort from sunburned skin. When oatmeal is soaked in water and applied to the skin as a paste, it can help to reduce itching and redness, and even help to prevent infection.  Vinegar can also help to alleviate sunburn symptoms. Apple cider vinegar, in particular, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help to reduce redness and prevent infection. When mixed with water and applied to sunburned skin, vinegar can help to cool and soothe the affected area.  Cool compresses made from cold water can also provide relief from sunburn. When a cool compress is applied to sunburned skin, it can help to reduce heat and discomfort, and even help to prevent peeling. This is because cold water helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which can make sunburn feel less painful.  Lastly, yogurt has been used as a natural remedy for sunburn for centuries. Yogurt contains lactic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can help to soothe and calm sunburned skin. When applied to sunburned skin, yogurt can help to reduce redness and itching, and even help to promote healing.
supplementary_related_ailments: When people get sunburned, they may feel other painful symptoms besides the red, sore skin. One common problem is dehydration, which can be relieved with aloe vera juice. Aloe vera has cooling properties that help bring down the swelling and reduce the risk of blisters. It also replenishes fluids and soothes the skin.  Another issue sunburn victims often face is fever, which can be cooled down with fever-reducing herbs like willow bark. Willow bark contains salicylic acid, similar to aspirin, which helps lower body temperature and ease headaches.  Some people may experience sunburn-induced rashes or itchy skin, which can be calmed with cooling herbs like chamomile tea. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and itching, making it easier to sleep and relax.  Additionally, sunburn can lead to headaches and fatigue, which can be alleviated with herbs like ginger and peppermint. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help ease pain and reduce nausea, while peppermint oil can help ease headaches and improve digestion.  Sunburned skin can also be dry and cracked, which can be treated with moisturizing herbs like calendula and comfrey. Calendula has antiseptic properties that help speed up the healing process and reduce the risk of infection, while comfrey can help repair damaged skin tissues.  These medicinal herbs can help alleviate the symptoms associated with sunburn and promote faster recovery, but it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using them, especially if the sunburn is severe or accompanied by other health issues.
